---
title: "Revisions_SurfacesCode"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-13"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Load packages
library(readr)      
library(tidyverse)
library(xtable)
library(readxl)       
library(dplyr)      
library(ggplot2)     
library(tidyr)       
library(purrr)       
library(stringr)     
library(forcats)     
library(broom)       
library(knitr)       
library(kableExtra)  
library(ggpubr)
library(rstatix)
library(car) 
library(scales) 
library(mgcv)
library(brms)
library(effsize)
library(patchwork)
library(emmeans)
```

```{r}
# Load processed data
reproduction <- readRDS("reproduction.rds") # Reproduction/growth
surfaces <- readRDS("surfaces.rds") # Survival 
merged_data <- readRDS("merged_data.rds")
```


```{r}
#function to create data structure overview
create_data_overview <- function(data, dataset_name) {
  data.frame(
    Variable = names(data),
    Type = sapply(data, class)
  ) %>%
    kable(caption = dataset_name) %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, 
                  bootstrap_options = "striped",
                  position = "left")
}

# Show structure of both datasets
create_data_overview(reproduction, "Reproduction Dataset Structure")
create_data_overview(surfaces, "Surfaces Dataset Structure")
```


```{r}

#Phage survival across different surfaces
#Figure S1: Changes in survival rates for T4 and PhiX174 over time on different surfaces.

#Define custom colors
custom_colors <- c("Control" = "#1f77b4",
                   "Copper" = "#ff7f0e",
                   "Plastic" = "#2ca02c",
                   "Steel" = "#9932CC")

phagesurvivalPlot <- ggplot(surfaces, aes(x = Time, y = log10(VirusTiter), color = Surface, shape = Surface)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = interaction(Surface, Experiment)), alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_grid(PhageType ~ Temperature, scales = "free_y",
             labeller = labeller(Temperature = function(x) paste0(x, "C"))) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17, 15, 18)) +
  labs(#title = "Phage Survival on Different Surfaces and Temperatures",
       x = "Time (hours)",
       y = expression(log[10]~"Virus Titer"),
       color = "Surface",
       shape = "Surface") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "grey80", fill = NA),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
  ) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1, override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1)),
         shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1, override.aes = list(size = 4, alpha = 1)))

#Saving the plot
png("PhageSurvivalPlot.png", width=6, height=4, units="in", res=300)
print(phagesurvivalPlot)
dev.off() #png form

ggsave("PhageSurvivalPlot.pdf", plot = phagesurvivalPlot, width = 6, height = 4, units = "in") #pdf form
```

```{r}
#Non-parametric tests
# Non-parametric analysis
# Calculate survival rate relative to initial titer (time 0)
survival_data <- surfaces %>%
  group_by(Experiment, PhageType, Temperature, Surface) %>%
  mutate(
    InitialTiter = VirusTiter[Time == 0],
    SurvivalRate = VirusTiter / InitialTiter * 100
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Function to check normality and homogeneity of variance
check_assumptions <- function(data, surface) {
  filtered_data <- data %>%
    filter(Surface == surface, Time == 8)  # Looking at final timepoint
  
  # Shapiro test for each group with error handling
  normality_tests <- filtered_data %>%
    group_by(PhageType, Temperature) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      mean = mean(SurvivalRate),
      sd = sd(SurvivalRate),
      shapiro_p = tryCatch(
        shapiro.test(SurvivalRate)$p.value,
        error = function(e) NA
      )
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      normality_status = case_when(
        is.na(shapiro_p) & sd == 0 ~ "Constant values",
        is.na(shapiro_p) ~ "Test failed",
        shapiro_p > 0.05 ~ "Normal",
        TRUE ~ "Non-normal"
      )
    )
    # Levene's test with error handling
  levene_result <- tryCatch(
    car::leveneTest(SurvivalRate ~ interaction(PhageType, Temperature), 
                    data = filtered_data),
    error = function(e) "Levene's test failed - likely due to constant values"
  )
  
  list(normality = normality_tests, variance = levene_result)
}

write.csv(survival_data, "survival_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}
# Function for non-parametric analysis
analyze_surface <- function(data, surface_name) {
  surface_data <- data %>%
    filter(Surface == surface_name, Time == 8)  # Analysis at final timepoint
  
  # Kruskal-Wallis test for each factor
  kw_phage <- kruskal.test(SurvivalRate ~ PhageType, data = surface_data)
  kw_temp <- kruskal.test(SurvivalRate ~ Temperature, data = surface_data)
  
  # Wilcoxon rank sum tests for pairwise comparisons
  pairwise_phage <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
    surface_data$SurvivalRate,
    interaction(surface_data$PhageType, surface_data$Temperature),
    p.adjust.method = "bonferroni"
  )
  
  list(
    kw_phage = kw_phage,
    kw_temp = kw_temp,
    pairwise = pairwise_phage
  )
}

# Check assumptions for each surface
surfaces_list <- unique(surfaces$Surface)
assumption_results <- lapply(surfaces_list, function(s) check_assumptions(survival_data, s))
names(assumption_results) <- surfaces_list
```


```{r}

# Create visualization of survival curves
create_survival_plot <- function(data, surface_name) {
  ggplot(data %>% filter(Surface == surface_name), 
         aes(x = Time, y = SurvivalRate + 0.01,  
             color = interaction(PhageType, Temperature, sep = " "),
             shape = interaction(PhageType, Temperature, sep = " "))) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 2) +
    stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line") +
    stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
    scale_y_log10(labels = scales::label_number()) +
    labs(
      x = "Time (hours)", 
      y = "Survival Rate (%)", 
      color = "Phage Type & Temperature",
      shape = "Phage Type & Temperature"
    ) +
    theme_pubr() +
    ggtitle(paste("Virus Survival Rate on", surface_name)) +
    theme(legend.position = "right")
}

#Saving the plot
png("SurvivalPlot.png", width=6, height=4, units="in", res=300)
print(create_survival_plot)
dev.off() #png form
# Create plots for each surface
survival_plots <- lapply(surfaces_list, function(s) create_survival_plot(survival_data, s))
names(survival_plots) <- surfaces_list

# Perform analysis for each surface
statistical_results <- lapply(surfaces_list, function(s) analyze_surface(survival_data, s))
names(statistical_results) <- surfaces_list

# Print results for inspection
print("Assumption check results:")
print(assumption_results)

print("Statistical analysis results:")
print(statistical_results)


#Table S4 Kruskal--Wallis .
```


```{r}
# Create summary statistics table
survival_summary <- survival_data %>%
  group_by(Surface, PhageType, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    Initial_Titer = mean(VirusTiter[Time == 0]),
    Final_Titer = mean(VirusTiter[Time == 8]),
    Survival_Rate = mean(SurvivalRate[Time == 8]),
    SD = sd(SurvivalRate[Time == 8])
  ) %>%
  arrange(Surface, Temperature, PhageType)

# Create formatted table
survival_table <- survival_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Initial_Titer = formatC(Initial_Titer, format = "e", digits = 2),
    Final_Titer = formatC(Final_Titer, format = "e", digits = 2),
    Survival_Rate = round(Survival_Rate, 2),
    SD = round(SD, 2)
  ) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Summary of Virus Survival Rates by Surface and Temperature",
    col.names = c("Surface", "Phage", "Temp (°C)", 
                  "Initial Titer", "Final Titer", 
                  "Survival Rate (%)", "SD"),
    format = "html"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  group_rows(index = table(survival_summary$Surface))

#Table S2 Summary of virus survival rates.
```

```{r}
#Reviewer 2: Convert table into figure

#Figure 3: Survival rates at final time-points.

# Ensure factor ordering matches the table (Control, Copper, Plastic, Steel)
# and phage labels use the Greek phi.
plot_data <- survival_summary %>%
  mutate(
    Surface     = factor(Surface, levels = c("Control", "Copper", "Plastic", "Steel")),
    PhageType   = factor(PhageType,
                         levels = c("PhiX174", "T4"),
                         labels = c("φX174", "T4")),  
    Temperature = factor(Temperature,
                         levels = c(4, 37),
                         labels = c("4 °C", "37 °C"))
  )

#Grouped bar chart with SD error bars

fig_bar <- ggplot(plot_data,
                  aes(x = Surface, y = Survival_Rate,
                      fill = PhageType)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
           width = 0.7, colour = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(Survival_Rate - SD, 0),
                    ymax = pmin(Survival_Rate + SD, 100)),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
                width = 0.25, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f", Survival_Rate),
                y = pmin(Survival_Rate + SD, 100) + 3),
            position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
            size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ Temperature) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("φX174" = "#4C8DBF",
                               "T4"        = "#D9534F")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110),
                     breaks = seq(0, 100, 20),
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.02))) +
  labs(x = "Surface",
       y = "Survival rate at 8 h (%)",
       fill = "Phage") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
        strip.background   = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
        legend.position    = "top")

ggsave("Figure_survival_summary_bar.pdf", fig_bar, width = 8, height = 4.5)
ggsave("Figure_survival_summary_bar.png", fig_bar, width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 300)

```


```{r}
# Calculate rate of decline for each condition
rate_analysis <- survival_data %>%
  group_by(Surface, PhageType, Temperature, Experiment) %>%
  do(model = lm(log10(SurvivalRate + 0.01) ~ Time, data = .)) %>%
  mutate(
    slope = coef(model)[2],
    r_squared = summary(model)$r.squared,
    p_value = summary(model)$coefficients[2,4]
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Surface, PhageType, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_slope = mean(slope),
    sd_slope = sd(slope),
    mean_r2 = mean(r_squared),
    n = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    half_life = -log(2)/(mean_slope * log(10)),
    decline_rate_per_hour = -(1 - 10^mean_slope) * 100
  )
```


```{r}
# Create formatted table of decline rates
decline_table <- rate_analysis %>%
  mutate(
    mean_slope = round(mean_slope, 4),
    sd_slope = round(sd_slope, 4),
    mean_r2 = round(mean_r2, 3),
    half_life = round(half_life, 2),
    decline_rate_per_hour = round(decline_rate_per_hour, 2)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Surface, Temperature, PhageType) %>%
  kable(
    caption = "Rate of Decline Analysis by Surface, Temperature, and Phage Type",
    col.names = c("Surface", "Phage", "Temp (°C)", 
                  "Log10 Slope", "Slope SD", "R²",
                  "n", "Half-life (h)", "Decline Rate (%/h)"),
    format = "html"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE) %>%
  group_rows(index = table(rate_analysis$Surface))

# Display results
print(decline_table)
```


```{r}
#Reviwer comment:
#Run the two factorial model instead: Throughout the entire manuscript, the authors state that their analyses reveal interactions between environmental variables (E×E), i.e., surface type × temperature interactions, and they argue that these interactions are phage-dependent (e.g., in line 181 they mention “virus type by environment interactions”, G×E). I am not sure that the statistical analyses they performed are most appropriate for quantifying these interactions. Why were separate tests performed for each surface, with phage type and temperature as the variables? It would seem appropriate to instead fit a model linking SurvivalRate to all three variables (phage type, surface type, and temperature) as the independent variables. My understanding is that the authors should have enough data for such a model to converge:  

#SurvivalRate = 𝛼 + 𝜏 Phage + 𝜃 Surface + 𝛾 Temperature + (𝜏𝜃) Phage × Surface + (𝜏𝛾) Phage × Temperature 

#+ (𝜃𝛾) Surface × Temperature + (𝜏𝜃𝛾) Phage × Surface × Temperature + 𝜀  

#(In the equation above, note that I’m using notation similar to that in equation 2 of the manuscript, even if it is somewhat non-standard). In my view, this model would provide a more clear and direct quantification of E×E, G×E, and G×E×E interactions.  

# DATA: final timepoint only
final_data <- survival_data %>%
  filter(Time == 8) %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType    = factor(PhageType),
    Surface      = factor(Surface),
    Temperature  = factor(Temperature)
  )


# FULL FACTORIAL MODEL
# SurvivalRate ~ Phage * Surface * Temperature

final_data <- survival_data %>%
  filter(Time == 8) %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType   = factor(PhageType),
    Surface     = factor(Surface),
    Temperature = factor(Temperature)
  )

fit_full <- lm(SurvivalRate ~ PhageType * Surface * Temperature,
               data = final_data)

anova_full <- Anova(fit_full, type = 3)
```


```{r}
#Build a table for the results of the model and ANOVA
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(is.na(p), NA_character_,
         ifelse(p < 0.001, "< 0.001",
                sprintf("%.3f", p)))
}

# Format table
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  ifelse(is.na(p), NA_character_,
         ifelse(p < 0.001, "< 0.001",
                sprintf("%.3f", p)))
}

# build table
tab_pub <- broom::tidy(anova_full) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)", term != "Residuals") %>%
  mutate(
    Effect = case_when(
      term == "PhageType" ~ "Phage",
      term == "Surface" ~ "Surface",
      term == "Temperature" ~ "Temperature",
      term == "PhageType:Surface" ~ "Phage $\\times$ Surface",
      term == "PhageType:Temperature" ~ "Phage $\\times$ Temperature",
      term == "Surface:Temperature" ~ "Surface $\\times$ Temperature",
      term == "PhageType:Surface:Temperature" ~ "Phage $\\times$ Surface $\\times$ Temperature",
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    df1 = df,
    df2 = fit_full$df.residual,
    `F(df1, df2)` = sprintf("F(%d, %d) = %.2f", df1, df2, statistic),
    `p-value` = fmt_p(p.value)
  ) %>%
  select(Effect, `F(df1, df2)`, `p-value`)

latex_code <- knitr::kable(
  tab_pub,
  format = "latex",
  booktabs = TRUE,
  escape = FALSE,
  caption = "Type III ANOVA for survival rate at the final timepoint (Time = 8) from the full factorial model: SurvivalRate $\\sim$ Phage $\\times$ Surface $\\times$ Temperature.",
  label = "tab:anova_survival_full"
)

cat(latex_code)
```

```{r}

# Set emmeans options so CIs are 95% and p-values use Tukey adjustment
emm_options(opt.digits = FALSE)

# ESTIMATED MARGINAL MEANS (every factor combination)

# Reports the model-estimated mean survival rate for each
# Phage x Surface x Temperature cell, with 95% CIs.

emm_all <- emmeans(fit_full, ~ PhageType * Surface * Temperature)

emm_all_df <- as.data.frame(emm_all) %>%
  rename(EMM = emmean, SE = SE, df = df,
         lower.CI = lower.CL, upper.CI = upper.CL) %>%
  mutate(
    lower.CI = pmax(lower.CI, 0),       
    upper.CI = pmin(upper.CI, 100),    
    across(c(EMM, SE, lower.CI, upper.CI), ~ round(.x, 3))
  )

# Table for supplementary
emm_all_table <- emm_all_df %>%
  kable(caption = "Supplementary Table SX. Estimated marginal means (EMMs) of survival rate at the final timepoint for each Phage x Surface x Temperature combination, with 95% confidence intervals.",
        booktabs = TRUE) %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position", "scale_down"))

print(emm_all_table)
```


```{r}
# 1. Data frames as CSV in Tables
dir.create("Tables", showWarnings = FALSE)
write.csv(survival_summary, "Tables/survival_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(rate_analysis, "Tables/rate_analysis.csv", row.names = FALSE)


# 2. Lists or printed results as TXT 
assumption_text <- capture.output(print(assumption_results))
writeLines(assumption_text, "Tables/assumption_results.txt")

statistical_text <- capture.output(print(statistical_results))
writeLines(statistical_text, "Tables/statistical_results.txt")

decline_table_text <- capture.output(print(decline_table))
writeLines(decline_table_text, "Tables/decline_table.txt")

survival_table_text <- capture.output(print(survival_table))
writeLines(survival_table_text, "Tables/survival_table.txt")
```

```{r}

#Figure 2: Phage survival across surfaces.

#Plot Survival rates (a)

# Survival rate separate figure
df <- read.csv("Tables/survival_summary.csv")
df <- df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C")))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp,
    levels = df %>%
      arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>%
      distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
      pull(Surface_Temp)
  ))


theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.9),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key.width = unit(1.8, "cm"),   
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),  
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.box = "horizontal"
  )


line_types <- c("solid", "dashed")
names(line_types) <- unique(df$PhageType)

phage_colors <- c("PhiX174" = "black", "T4" = "black")

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.4)

SurvivalratePlot <- ggplot(df, aes(
  x = Surface_Temp,
  y = Survival_Rate,
  shape = PhageType
)) +
  geom_point(
    position = pd,
    size = 2,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = Survival_Rate - SD,
        ymax = Survival_Rate + SD),
    width = 0.2,
    position = pd,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(
      "PhiX174" = 16,  # solid circle
      "T4" = 17        # solid triangle
    ),
    labels = c(
      "PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"),
      "T4" = "T4"
    ),
    name = "Phage Type"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Survival rate (%)",
    title = "Survival Rate"
  ) +
  theme_nature
SurvivalratePlot


 ggsave("SurvivalRatePlot.png", SurvivalratePlot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)
```


```{r}

# Reviewer: Fig 2. It is not possible to differentiate between the symbols for X174 and T4 (i.e. the circle and triangle) for many points the figure, especially when there are small error bars (see 2b Steel 4C, for example). Please correct.
#Add color to show differences

#Plot Survival rates (a)
# Survival rate separate figure

df <- read.csv("Tables/survival_summary.csv")
df <- df %>% 
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = FALSE))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C")))
df <- df %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp,
    levels = df %>%
      arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>%
      distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
      pull(Surface_Temp)
  ))


theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.9),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center"
  )

phage_colors <- c("PhiX174" = "#1b7837", "T4" = "#762a83")
pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.4)

SurvivalratePlot <- ggplot(df, aes(
  x = Surface_Temp,
  y = Survival_Rate,
  shape = PhageType,
  color = PhageType
)) +
  geom_point(position = pd, size = 2.5) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = Survival_Rate - SD, ymax = Survival_Rate + SD),
    width = 0.2, position = pd, linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("PhiX174" = 16, "T4" = 17),
    labels = c("PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"), "T4" = "T4")
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = phage_colors,
    labels = c("PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"), "T4" = "T4")
  ) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, linetype = 0))) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Survival rate (%)",
    title = "Survival Rate"
  ) +
  theme_nature

SurvivalratePlot


 ggsave("SurvivalRatePlot.png", SurvivalratePlot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)
```


```{r}

#Decline rate figure without connecting lines and with error bars

# Load + prep (same as half-life)
df <- read.csv("Tables/rate_analysis.csv") %>%
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType = factor(PhageType),
    Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C"))
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Surface_Temp = factor(
      Surface_Temp,
      levels = df %>% arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>% distinct(Surface_Temp) %>% pull(Surface_Temp)
    )
  )

# Decline rate error bars based on slope ± SD (propagated)
df_plot_decline <- df %>%
  mutate(
    slope_mean = mean_slope,
    slope_low  = mean_slope - sd_slope,
    slope_high = mean_slope + sd_slope,

    decline_mean = -(1 - 10^slope_mean) * 100,
    decline_low  = -(1 - 10^slope_low)  * 100,
    decline_high = -(1 - 10^slope_high) * 100,

    decline_lwr = pmin(decline_low, decline_high),
    decline_upr = pmax(decline_low, decline_high)
  )

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.4)

declineRate_plot <- ggplot(df_plot_decline, aes(
  x = Surface_Temp,
  y = decline_mean,
  shape = PhageType,
  color = PhageType        
)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = decline_lwr, ymax = decline_upr),
    width = 0.20,
    position = pd,
    linewidth = 1.0
                          
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = pd,
    size = 2
                            
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("PhiX174" = 16, "T4" = 17),
    labels = c("PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"), "T4" = "T4"),
    name = "Phage Type"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(        
    values = c("PhiX174" = "#1b7837", "T4" = "#762a83"),
    labels = c("PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"), "T4" = "T4"),
    name = "Phage Type"
  ) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, linetype = 0))) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Decline rate (per hour)",
    title = "Decline rate"
  ) +
  theme_nature

declineRate_plot


 ggsave("DeclineRate.png", declineRate_plot, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}

#Half life plot with error bars
df <- read.csv("Tables/rate_analysis.csv") %>%
  filter(!grepl("control", Surface, ignore.case = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType = factor(PhageType),
    Surface_Temp = paste(Surface, paste0(Temperature, "°C"))
  )

df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    Surface_Temp = factor(
      Surface_Temp,
      levels = df %>% arrange(Surface, Temperature) %>% distinct(Surface_Temp) %>% pull(Surface_Temp)
    )
  )

# Convert mean_slope ± sd_slope into half-life bounds (±1 SD on k)
ln2 <- log(2)

df_plot <- df %>%
  mutate(
    k_mean = -mean_slope,
    k_low  = pmax(k_mean - sd_slope, 1e-12),  
    k_high = k_mean + sd_slope,
    half_life_mean = ln2 / k_mean,
    half_life_low  = ln2 / k_high,  
    half_life_high = ln2 / k_low   
  )


pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.4)

Half_Life2 <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(
  x = Surface_Temp,
  y = half_life_mean,
  shape = PhageType,
  color = PhageType         
)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = half_life_low, ymax = half_life_high),
    width = 0.20,
    position = pd,
    linewidth = 1.0
                            
  ) +
  geom_point(
    position = pd,
    size = 3
                            
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c("PhiX174" = 16, "T4" = 17),
    labels = c("PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"), "T4" = "T4"),
    name = "Phage Type"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(        
    values = c("PhiX174" = "#1b7837", "T4" = "#762a83"),
    labels = c("PhiX174" = expression(phi * "X174"), "T4" = "T4"),
    name = "Phage Type"
  ) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, linetype = 0))) +
  labs(
    x = "Environment (Surface × Temperature)",
    y = "Half-life (hours)",
    title = "Half-life"
  ) +
  theme_nature

Half_Life2


 ggsave("Halflives.png", Half_Life2, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
#Differences in growth rates

#Effect of surface and temperature on reproduction 

# Data preparation and initial analysis

reproduction_analysis <- reproduction %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, InactivationTimeHours) %>%
  arrange(ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  mutate(
    # Calculate normalized titer (relative to starting point)
    NormalizedTiter = VirusTiter / first(VirusTiter),
    # Calculate growth rate between timepoints
    GrowthRate = case_when(
      ReproductionTimeMinutes > 0 ~ (VirusTiter - first(VirusTiter)) / ReproductionTimeMinutes,
      TRUE ~ 0
    ),
    # Calculate log-transformed titer for easier visualization
    LogTiter = log10(VirusTiter + 1)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```



```{r}
#Remake figure - normalized growth plot

#Figure S2: T4 and PhiX174 growth after exposure to surfaces and temperatures

calc_se <- function(x) sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(x)))

library(scales)

LOD <- 1   # limit of detection (PFU/mL)
growth_plot <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  summarise(
    MeanTiter = mean(VirusTiter, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(MeanTiter_plot = pmax(MeanTiter, LOD / 2)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ReproductionTimeMinutes,
             y = MeanTiter_plot,
             color = PhageType,
             group = PhageType)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_log10(
    labels = scientific,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.05))
  ) +
  facet_grid(InactivationTemp ~ Surface,
             labeller = labeller(InactivationTemp = function(x) paste0(x, " °C"))) +
  theme_pubr() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (minutes)",
    y = "Virus Titer (PFU/mL)"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

ggsave("growth_plotLOD.png", growth_plot, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```




```{r}
# Normalization
# Substitute below-LOD baseline values with LOD/2 to avoid Inf values
# When computing fold-change (Titer / baseline).
#  Adjust the group_by keys to match your replicate structure.

LOD <- 1  

reproduction_analysis <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, InactivationTimeHours) %>%
  mutate(
    VirusTiter_adj  = ifelse(is.na(VirusTiter) | VirusTiter < LOD, LOD/2, VirusTiter),
    baseline_adj    = VirusTiter_adj[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 0],
    NormalizedTiter = VirusTiter_adj / baseline_adj
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Max-normalize each time-course (Phage x Surface x Temperature) to its own peak titer. Each curve will run from a low value to 1.0.

plot_summary <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
  mutate(
    PeakTiter            = max(VirusTiter, na.rm = TRUE),
    NormalizedTiter      = VirusTiter / PeakTiter
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp, ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  summarise(
    MeanNorm = mean(NormalizedTiter, na.rm = TRUE),
    SE       = calc_se(NormalizedTiter),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )
```




```{r}

# Plot normalized titer (fraction of peak) over reproduction time
#Figure S3: Normalized growth dynamics.

normalized_plot <- plot_summary %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ReproductionTimeMinutes,
             y = MeanNorm,
             color = PhageType,
             group = PhageType)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  facet_grid(InactivationTemp ~ Surface,
             labeller = labeller(InactivationTemp = function(x) paste0(x, " °C"))) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1.1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2),
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 30, 40, 50, 60)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Phix" = "#E64B35", "T4" = "#4DBBD5")) +
  labs(
    x     = "Reproduction time (minutes)",
    y     = "Normalized titer (% of peak)",
    color = "Phage"
  ) +
  theme_pubr(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
    legend.position  = "top",
    plot.margin      = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)
  )

print(normalized_plot)

ggsave("reproduction_normalized.png", normalized_plot, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```


```{r}
#Final comparisons on normalized data
final_comparison <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60,
         InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
  summarise(
    T4_Mean = mean(VirusTiter[PhageType == "T4"]),
    Phix_Mean = mean(VirusTiter[PhageType == "Phix"]),
    T4_SE = calc_se(VirusTiter[PhageType == "T4"]),
    Phix_SE = calc_se(VirusTiter[PhageType == "Phix"]),
    TiterRatio = T4_Mean / Phix_Mean,
    LogFoldDifference = log2(TiterRatio),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
```


```{r}
# Growth phase analysis
growth_phases <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
  summarise(
    EarlyGrowthRate = (VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 30] - VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 0]) / 30,
    LateGrowthRate = (VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60] - VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 30]) / 30,
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = PhageType,
    values_from = c(EarlyGrowthRate, LateGrowthRate)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    EarlyGrowthRatio = EarlyGrowthRate_T4 / EarlyGrowthRate_Phix,
    LateGrowthRatio = LateGrowthRate_T4 / LateGrowthRate_Phix
  )
```


```{r}
# Temperature sensitivity analysis
temp_sensitivity <- reproduction_analysis %>%
  filter(ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60,
         InactivationTimeHours == 0) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface) %>%
  summarise(
    TempEffect = mean(VirusTiter[InactivationTemp == 37]) / mean(VirusTiter[InactivationTemp == 4]),
    LogTempEffect = log2(TempEffect),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )
```


```{r}
# Combine all metrics into final table
final_table <- final_comparison %>%
  left_join(growth_phases, by = c("Surface", "InactivationTemp")) %>%
  select(Surface, InactivationTemp, TiterRatio, LogFoldDifference, EarlyGrowthRatio, LateGrowthRatio) %>%
  arrange(Surface, InactivationTemp)

#Print growth phases for overleaf

#scientific notation
sci_cols <- c("EarlyGrowthRate_Phix",
              "EarlyGrowthRate_T4",
              "LateGrowthRate_Phix",
              "LateGrowthRate_T4")

growth_sci <- growth_phases %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(sci_cols),
                ~ formatC(., format = "e", digits = 2)))
# Create xtable
growth_tab <- xtable(growth_sci)

#Print

print(
  growth_tab,
  include.rownames = FALSE,
  booktabs = TRUE,
  floating = FALSE,                 # <--- important
  sanitize.text.function = identity
)
```
```{r}
#Summary stats for growth/reproduction data 
#Prepare data for growth main graphs
df <- reproduction
pc <- 1

df_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(Surface, InactivationTemp, PhageType) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_log = mean(log10(VirusTiter + pc), na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_log   = sd(log10(VirusTiter + pc), na.rm = TRUE),
    n        = sum(!is.na(VirusTiter)),
    se_log   = sd_log / sqrt(n),
    ci_mult  = qt(0.975, df = n - 1),
    lwr_log  = mean_log - ci_mult * se_log,
    upr_log  = mean_log + ci_mult * se_log,
    mean_rep = 10^mean_log - pc,
    ymin     = pmax(10^lwr_log - pc, 1e-6),
    ymax     = pmax(10^upr_log - pc, 1e-6),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Surface_Temp = paste0(Surface, " ", InactivationTemp, "°C")
  )
```


```{r}

#Figure 4: PhiX174 and T4 growth dynamics after exposure to surface x temperature combinations.

# Factor order
surface_levels <- df_summary %>%
  arrange(Surface, InactivationTemp) %>%
  distinct(Surface_Temp) %>%
  pull(Surface_Temp)

df_summary <- df_summary %>%
  mutate(Surface_Temp = factor(Surface_Temp, levels = surface_levels))

df_phi <- df_summary %>% filter(PhageType == "Phix")
df_t4  <- df_summary %>% filter(PhageType == "T4")

theme_nature <- theme_bw(base_size = 14, base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.9),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.6),
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.key = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = "center"
  )


base_theme <- theme_nature +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    plot.title   = element_text(face = "plain", hjust = 0.5, size = 14),
    axis.title.x = element_blank()
  )

p_phi <- ggplot(df_phi, aes(Surface_Temp, mean_rep)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
                width = 0.28, linewidth = 1.0, color = "black") +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, color = "black") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = expression(phi * "X174: Growth after surface exposure"),
    y     = expression("Mean titer (log"[10]*" PFU/mL)")
  ) +
  base_theme

p_t4 <- ggplot(df_t4, aes(Surface_Temp, mean_rep)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax),
                width = 0.25, linewidth = 1.0, color = "black") +
  geom_point(size = 2.8, color = "black") +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    title = "T4: Growth after surface exposure",
    y     = expression("Mean titer (log"[10]*" PFU/mL)"),
    x     = "Surface and Temperature"
  ) +
  theme_nature +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    plot.title  = element_text(face = "plain", hjust = 0.5, size = 14)
  )

p_stacked <- p_phi / p_t4 + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1))


ggsave("Growth_stacked_with_CI.png", p_stacked, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 600)
p_stacked
```


```{r}
#Relationship between survival and growth
df <- merged_data %>%
  mutate(
    PhageType = factor(PhageType),
    Surface = factor(Surface),
    Temperature = as.numeric(Temperature),
    Time = as.numeric(Time),
    ReproductionTimeMinutes = as.numeric(ReproductionTimeMinutes),
    MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces = as.numeric(MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces),
    VirusTiter = as.numeric(VirusTiter)
  )

#Correlate growth and survival at the summary level. Then compute survival at 8hr and final titer growth titer at 60 min per condition, then correlate across conditions.
condition_summary <- df %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Temperature, Surface) %>%
  summarise(
    final_survival      = mean(MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces[Time == 8],
                               na.rm = TRUE),
    final_reproduction  = mean(VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60],
                               na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cor.test(condition_summary$final_survival,
         condition_summary$final_reproduction,
         method = "spearman")
```


```{r}
# Aggregate to one survival + one growth value per
#  (PhageType, Surface, Temperature, InactivationTimeHours).
#  5 properly paired observations per cell.
cell_pairs <- df %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, Temperature, Time) %>%
  summarise(
    survival_titer        = mean(MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces, na.rm = TRUE),
    reproduction_final    = mean(VirusTiter[ReproductionTimeMinutes == 60],
                                 na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```



```{r}
#  Per-cell Spearman correlations (n = 5 per cell, pairing by inactivation time)

spearman_per_cell1 <- cell_pairs %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    cor_result = list(suppressWarnings(
                   cor.test(survival_titer, reproduction_final,
                            method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
                 )),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    rho     = map_dbl(cor_result, ~ .x$estimate),
    p.value = map_dbl(cor_result, ~ .x$p.value),
    sig     = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ ""
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-cor_result)

print(spearman_per_cell1)
```
```{r}
#Print for overleaf
# Format data
# Print for overleaf
formatted_table <- spearman_per_cell1 %>%
  mutate(
    p.value = signif(p.value, 3),
    rho     = round(rho, 3)
  )

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(
  formatted_table,
  caption = "Spearman Correlation Results Between Survival and Single Growth Timepoint",
  align   = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "c"),
  digits  = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 3, 3, 0)
)

print(
  xtable_output,
  include.rownames      = FALSE,
  floating              = TRUE,
  latex.environments    = "center",
  caption.placement     = "top",
  sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x)
)
```


```{r}
# Overall correlation across all 16 conditions
condition_summary <- cell_pairs %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    final_survival     = mean(survival_titer,     na.rm = TRUE),
    final_reproduction = mean(reproduction_final, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cor.test(condition_summary$final_survival,
         condition_summary$final_reproduction,
         method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)

# Split by phage and  report two separate overall correlations
condition_summary %>%
  group_by(PhageType) %>%
  summarise(
    overall_rho     = cor(final_survival, final_reproduction, method = "spearman"),
    overall_p_value = cor.test(final_survival, final_reproduction,
                               method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)$p.value,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```



```{r}

LOD <- 1   # plaque assay limit of detection (PFU/mL)

# Per-(Phage x Surface x Temperature x InactivationTime) summaries
#    using ALL 5 reproduction timepoints. n = 5 paired obs per cell.
cell_pairs <- df %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, Temperature,
           Time, ReproductionTimeMinutes) %>%
  summarise(
    VirusTiter           = mean(VirusTiter, na.rm = TRUE),
    SurvivalTiter        = mean(MeanSurfaceVirusTiter_surfaces, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, Temperature, Time) %>%
  arrange(ReproductionTimeMinutes, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    survival_titer = first(SurvivalTiter),  # one value per IT_h

    # Option A — geometric mean of titer across all 5 reproduction timepoints
    reproduction_mean_log = mean(log10(pmax(VirusTiter, LOD / 2))),

    .groups = "drop"
  )
```


```{r}
# Per-cell Spearman correlations (still n = 5, but the reproduction value
#    now uses all 5 timepoints' worth of information)
spearman_per_cell <- cell_pairs %>%
  group_by(PhageType, Surface, Temperature) %>%
  summarise(
    n          = n(),
    cor_result = list(suppressWarnings(
                   cor.test(survival_titer, reproduction_mean_log,
                            method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)
                 )),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    rho     = map_dbl(cor_result, ~ .x$estimate),
    p.value = map_dbl(cor_result, ~ .x$p.value),
    sig     = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ ""
    )
  ) %>%
  select(-cor_result)

print(spearman_per_cell, n = Inf)

```


```{r}
#Print for overleaf
# Format data
# Print for overleaf
formatted_table <- spearman_per_cell %>%
  mutate(
    p.value = signif(p.value, 3),
    rho     = round(rho, 3)
  )

# Create and print xtable
xtable_output <- xtable(
  formatted_table,
  caption = "Spearman Correlation Results Between Survival and Reproduction",
  align   = c("l", "l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "c"),
  digits  = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 3, 3, 0)
)

print(
  xtable_output,
  include.rownames      = FALSE,
  floating              = TRUE,
  latex.environments    = "center",
  caption.placement     = "top",
  sanitize.text.function = function(x) gsub("_", "\\\\_", x)
)
```


```{r}

#Figure 5: Relationship between survival and growth.
#  Heatmap with rho values + significance stars

spearman_plot <- spearman_per_cell %>%
mutate(
  Surface     = factor(Surface, levels = c("Control", "Copper", "Plastic", "Steel")),
  Temperature = factor(Temperature, levels = c(4, 37),
                       labels = c("4 °C", "37 °C")),
  PhageType   = factor(PhageType,
                       levels = c("PhiX174", "T4"),
                       labels = c("φX174", "T4")),   # <-- add this line
  label       = paste0(sprintf("%.2f", rho),
                       ifelse(sig != "", paste0("\n", sig), ""))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Surface, y = Temperature, fill = rho)) +
  geom_tile(colour = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = label),
            size = 4, fontface = "bold", colour = "black", lineheight = 0.9) +
  facet_wrap(~ PhageType) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#3B4CC0", mid = "white", high = "#B40426",
                       midpoint = 0, limits = c(-1, 1),
                       name = "Spearman ρ") +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship between survival and growth",
    x     = "Surface",
    y     = "Temperature"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x      = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA),
    strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"),
    plot.title       = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid       = element_blank()
  )

print(spearman_plot)

#ggsave("Fig4_survival_reproduction_correlation.pdf", spearman_plot,
      # width = 8, height = 4)
ggsave("survival_reproduction_correlation.png", spearman_plot,
       width = 8, height = 4, dpi = 300)
```
